build_graph_element_classes.description: An authored root that contributes build declarations or compilable source membership.
An explicit target declaration creating a library, executable, interface target, or custom target.
A broad structural class of related targets such as products, tooling, validation, or packaging.
A binding from product identity to a target realization.
A configuration consumer surface that selects or constrains how the graph is configured.
A configuration consumer surface that records compiler, runtime, linker, and generator facts.
A build-time or configure-time verification surface that constrains graph legality.
A release or control-plane surface that consumes the graph but does not own it.
A downstream emitted build or distribution result that reflects the graph without becoming canonical build law.

target_classes.description: A target that realizes a registered product identity.
A library or support target that realizes runtime structure without being product identity.
A target for inspection, generation, validation, authoring, or operator support.
A test or audit target that validates behavior or structure.
A target that stages, packages, indexes, or verifies build outputs for distribution.
A target or helper flow that emits generated metadata, IDE projections, or configured descriptors.
An alias or aggregate target that provides stable handles or grouped invocation without creating new ownership.

source_build_ownership_rule_categories.rule: Source membership is defined by authored target declarations or authoritative helper modules, not by adjacency.
Product registration records a product-to-target mapping but does not replace source membership.
Alias targets point to ownership and realization; they do not create independent target ownership.
Generated outputs, manifests, and dist trees may reflect ownership but do not become the owning surface.
Build ownership is structural and must not be mistaken for semantic, domain, or runtime ownership.
Target matrix, component graph, install profiles, and release manifests consume the graph and may not redefine it.

canonical_build_surfaces: authored top-level and subordinate CMakeLists files
authored cmake helper modules that define graph structure, product registration, reproducibility, and dist support

product_platform_preset_toolchain_relati.platform_matrix_note: Target matrix rows describe supported products and platform constraints, but they are downstream release-control-plane consumers rather than build canon.
product_platform_preset_toolchain_relati.preset_note: Presets select configured lanes over the locked graph and must not silently define the graph.
product_platform_preset_toolchain_relati.toolchain_note: Toolchain descriptors record compiler and target facts and remain subordinate to authored build law.
product_platform_preset_toolchain_relati.runtime_alignment_note: Build grouping may realize kernel, component, service, product, and tooling layers, but must not flatten those runtime distinctions into build ontology.

validation_audit_categories.description: Verify target existence and assigned target class.
Verify explicit product registration and product-to-target realization.
Verify authored source membership and boundary constraints.
Verify that generated, dist, and manifest surfaces are not treated as canonical build law.
Verify product and platform-matrix consistency against the locked graph.
Verify preset and toolchain linkage stays subordinate to the locked graph.

prohibited_build_graph_shapes: preset files treated as hidden build canon
generated manifests treated as authoritative build truth
target names used as semantic ontology
product identity silently equated with one build realization
ownership guessed from convenience or adjacency
dist or archive outputs treated as source-of-truth build structure
downstream release registries redefining authored build law by convenience
runtime product, support, tooling, validation, and packaging targets flattened into one undifferentiated target vocabulary
